gi_status: Geographically Indicated; protected as cultural heritage of Mithila's artisan community
honest_framing: Designs in this catalog are Madhubani-INSPIRED. They are not authentic Mithila art. The living custodians of the authentic tradition are the artisans of Mithila — predominantly women working in family lineages. Customers who love these designs should also support practitioners directly through organizations like the Mithila Art Institute.

cited_influences.name: Sita Devi
cited_influences.what_we_draw_from: Mid-century kohbar wall-painting line grammar; the signature almond-eye treatment carried across our sets
cited_influences.use_in_engine: Cited in motif and master-painter tradition descriptions

cited_influences.name: Ganga Devi
cited_influences.what_we_draw_from: Bharni-style fill rhythm; the dot-and-petal ornamental aura around figures

cited_influences.name: Baua Devi
cited_influences.what_we_draw_from: Matsya (fish) and naga (serpent) compositional motifs visible in saddle decoration and ornamental panels

cited_influences.name: Mithila kohbar bridal-chamber tradition
cited_influences.style_school: Wall-painting tradition tied to Madhubani weddings
cited_influences.what_we_draw_from: Bird-and-animal motif vocabulary; the placement of decorative zones across figure bodies
cited_influences.use_in_engine: Cited in motif description for bird and animal subjects

madhubani-contemporary.intent: Translation of Mithila tradition adapted for contemporary apparel — restraint, vector-clean, mass-market premium

madhubani-master-painter.intent: Tradition rendered as if a Mithila master had painted it directly — hand-drawn line weight, composed palette, character-bearing eyes, controlled texture
madhubani-master-painter.default_for: premium tier, expressive pieces, gallery-grade designs
